message:
Define normalized data model for multi-agent conversation unification

Implement types.rs with serde-compatible structs representing the
canonical entity schema that all agent-specific connectors will
normalize into. This model abstracts over the heterogeneous formats
used by Codex (JSONL events), Claude Code (JSONL logs), Gemini (JSON
checkpoints), Cline (task JSON files), Amp (VS Code globalStorage),
and OpenCode (SQLite sessions).

Core types hierarchy:
- Agent: tool metadata (slug like "codex"/"cline", display name,
  version string, kind discriminator for Cli/VsCode/Hybrid agents)
- Workspace: project root path association (id, canonical absolute
  path, optional display name for UI presentation)
- Conversation: thread-level container (agent_slug reference, workspace
  link, external_id from source tool, title derived from first message
  or metadata, source_path to original log/DB file for jump-to-source
  UX, started_at/ended_at unix millis timestamps, approx_tokens for
  context length estimation, metadata_json blob for tool-specific
  extra fields, messages vec for in-memory conversation reconstruction)
- Message: individual turn (idx for ordering within conversation,
  role enum distinguishing User/Agent/Tool/System/Other, author string
  for multi-agent scenarios, created_at timestamp, content text,
  extra_json for tool-specific message metadata, snippets vec for
  embedded code references)
- Snippet: code fragment reference (optional file_path, start_line,
  end_line for range highlighting, language hint for syntax coloring,
  snippet_text for inline code blocks extracted from messages)
- Tag: classification label (id, name for user-defined or auto-inferred
  tags like "debugging", "refactoring", "blocked")

MessageRole enum design:
User/Agent/Tool/System as concrete variants for the common roles seen
across all tools, plus Other(String) variant for graceful handling of
novel role strings without parse failures. This Tagged union approach
(serialized as string via serde) enables round-trip preservation of
unusual roles while providing type-safe matching for the 95% case.

Serde derives on all types:
- Serialize + Deserialize for JSON import/export (insta snapshot tests,
  potential future export subcommand for conversation backup)
- Debug for tracing log output during connector development
- Clone for passing owned copies to async tasks (Conversation cloned
  into indexer work queue, Message cloned for Tantivy document builder)
- PartialEq/Eq on MessageRole and AgentKind for HashMap keys and test
  assertions

Schema alignment with SQLite storage layer:
These Rust structs directly mirror the table schema defined in
PLAN § 4.2 (agents, workspaces, conversations, messages, snippets,
tags, conversation_tags tables). Field names, types, and nullability
constraints match to enable straightforward rusqlite row mapping without
impedance mismatch. For example:
- Conversation.approx_tokens: Option<i64> maps to conversations.approx_tokens
  INTEGER NULL
- Message.extra_json: serde_json::Value maps to messages.extra_json TEXT
  (stored as JSON blob, parsed on read)
- Snippet.start_line/end_line: Option<i64> for snippets without line
  references (e.g., entire file contents)

PathBuf usage for filesystem paths:
Workspace.path, Conversation.workspace, Conversation.source_path, and
Snippet.file_path use PathBuf (not String) for platform-correct path
handling (backslashes on Windows, forward slashes on Unix) and integration
with std::fs APIs in connector scanning. Serde transparently serializes
PathBuf as strings in JSON representations.

i64 for all integer types (ids, timestamps, line numbers):
Matches SQLite INTEGER affinity (always stored as 64-bit signed) and
avoids narrowing conversions. Unix millisecond timestamps fit comfortably
in i64 range (2^63 / 1000 / 86400 / 365 ≈ 292 million years).

Metadata blobs (metadata_json, extra_json) as serde_json::Value:
Provides schema flexibility for tool-specific fields without polluting
the normalized model. For example:
- Codex metadata_json might contain {workspace_cwd, approval_count}
- Claude metadata_json might contain {project_id, api_conversation_id}
- Cline metadata_json might contain {task_id, provider: "anthropic"}
Connector implementations populate these; UI can optionally display
raw JSON in detail pane's "Raw JSON" tab (per PLAN § 6.2 layout).

This model is intentionally in-memory focused (owned Strings, Vecs, not
database-borrowed slices) because conversations are loaded in full for
display and indexing, not streamed. Memory cost is acceptable for typical
corpus size (PLAN § 12.3 baseline: 10k conversations, 1M messages fits
in <1GB RAM with string interning opportunities).

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 4.1 (conceptual
model), § 4.2 (SQLite schema), § 7.1 (NormalizedConversation struct
discussed, now implemented as Conversation), README.md (model/ module
overview).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+//! Normalized entity structs.
+
+use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
+use std::path::PathBuf;
+
+/// Roles seen across source agents.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub enum MessageRole {
+    User,
+    Agent,
+    Tool,
+    System,
+    Other(String),
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct Agent {
+    pub slug: String,
+    pub name: String,
+    pub version: Option<String>,
+    pub kind: AgentKind,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub enum AgentKind {
+    Cli,
+    VsCode,
+    Hybrid,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct Workspace {
+    pub id: i64,
+    pub path: PathBuf,
+    pub display_name: Option<String>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct Conversation {
+    pub id: i64,
+    pub agent_slug: String,
+    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,
+    pub external_id: Option<String>,
+    pub title: Option<String>,
+    pub source_path: PathBuf,
+    pub started_at: Option<i64>,
+    pub ended_at: Option<i64>,
+    pub approx_tokens: Option<i64>,
+    pub metadata_json: serde_json::Value,
+    pub messages: Vec<Message>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct Message {
+    pub idx: i64,
+    pub role: MessageRole,
+    pub author: Option<String>,
+    pub created_at: Option<i64>,
+    pub content: String,
+    pub extra_json: serde_json::Value,
+    pub snippets: Vec<Snippet>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct Snippet {
+    pub file_path: Option<PathBuf>,
+    pub start_line: Option<i64>,
+    pub end_line: Option<i64>,
+    pub language: Option<String>,
+    pub snippet_text: Option<String>,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct Tag {
+    pub id: i64,
+    pub name: String,
+}
